title: Validate scheduler package split and full test suite
description: Run targeted and full-suite validation for the package split, checking import compatibility, module/test size constraints, and end-to-end test parity after the scheduler and test decompositions land.
acceptance_criteria: `from agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler`, `SchedulerReporter`, and `SchedulerResult` works unchanged.
Scheduler package modules are each at or below 500 lines, and scheduler test files are each at or below 600 lines.
`uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes after the split.
Any regressions introduced by the refactor are either fixed or reported precisely.
conflict_risks:
Downstream beads that split core.py will have overlapping scope with `src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py`. They should be sequenced, not run in parallel.
Low. Only `tests/helpers.py` (new file) and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` (refactored) were modified. No source files touched.
None — no other in-progress beads touch these files.
None — `finalize.py` is a new file; `execution.py` changes are internal (method removal + delegation wiring). No public API surface changed.
No conflicts expected. Files touched are all within the scheduler package and were already claimed by this feature tree.
No file-scope conflicts expected; changes are isolated to scheduler package internals.
None — this bead only modified test files.
None. Only test files were modified; no source code was touched.
None — this bead only touches test files, not source files.
None — no source code changed, only test file reorganization.
tests/test_orchestrator.py reduced from 2917 to 2168 lines. Any sibling bead editing test_orchestrator.py should rebase on this change.
